page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 48.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization.

In benchmark testing, the m8azn.3xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics. Its single-threaded processing capabilities are further highlighted by top-tier results in gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and image processing. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes are strong, placing in the top 25%, which supports efficient data movement. Multi-core performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations perform at an average level relative to its resource class.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high-frequency compute capabilities and modern DDR5 memory without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O depends on network-attached EBS volumes, which is a key tradeoff for storage-heavy workloads. The server's baseline network bandwidth is 25 Gbps. This profile makes the m8azn.3xlarge highly suitable for high-performance general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, web serving, and light machine learning inference tasks such as text generation with small to medium models.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated 12-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 48.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network interface for EBS connectivity. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance, excellent single-threaded compression, and strong large-block memory bandwidth. While multi-core and database operations perform at average levels, the instance offers high compute density for its class. It is well-suited for web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 12-core AMD EPYC processor at 5.0 GHz, 48 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 25 Gbps network baseline, optimized for high-performance workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with a 12-core 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 48 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency dedicated processor instance featuring rapid DDR5 memory and optimized network throughput for demanding single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 12-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz
48.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS storage
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for web serving, single-threaded tasks, and light LLM inference
